title: CloudSentinel Risk Record
description: Schema for every risk record stored in DynamoDB cloudsentinel-risks table

riskTimestamp.description: Sort key. ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of when the risk was detected. Example: 2024-01-15T10:30:00+00:00

scanId.description: Optional UUID linking all risks from a single scan run

userId.description: Cognito user sub — used by the userId-module-index GSI for notification queries

module.description: Engineering domain that detected this risk

cloudProvider.description: Cloud provider the affected resource belongs to

resource.description: Resource type in human-readable form. Examples: S3 Bucket, EC2 Security Group, CI/CD Pipeline, API Gateway, DynamoDB Table, Glue ETL Job, Lambda Function

resourceName.description: Name or identifier of the specific resource instance

riskType.description: Short label for the risk class. Example: S3 Public Access Not Fully Blocked

riskReason.description: Explanation of why the configuration is risky

riskPriority.description: Severity classification. High = immediate action required. Medium = address soon. Low = recommended improvement.

remediationSteps.description: Ordered list of concrete actions to resolve the risk

alternativeSolutions.description: Secure alternative configurations or architectural approaches

aiExplanation.description: AI-generated plain-English explanation produced by the ai-explainer Lambda via Amazon Bedrock. Empty string until processed.

riskCategory.description: Category assigned by Amazon Comprehend keyword analysis

status.description: Resolution lifecycle state of the risk

notified.description: True after the notification-handler Lambda has sent an SNS alert for this risk. Prevents duplicate alerts.

region.description: AWS region where the risk was detected. Example: us-east-1

examples.riskType: S3 Public Access Not Fully Blocked
examples.riskReason: One or more Block Public Access settings are disabled on this bucket.
remediationSteps: Enable all four Block Public Access settings in the S3 console
Review and restrict the bucket policy for any public grants
alternativeSolutions: Use pre-signed URLs for any content that needs to be temporarily accessible
Serve static content through CloudFront with an Origin Access Control (OAC)
